content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Accepted all three amendments. The `--prompt-file` override path is internally inconsistent, nearby direct-relation status fallback can leak stale closed work when detail refresh is unavailable, and reviewer guidance currently advertises aliases that canonicalization later rejects.

## Votes

- `fr-23-f9f3069a`: Accept
  `execute_create_from_bead_in_dir` short-circuits nearby/dependency hydration whenever `prompt_override` is present and passes `NearbyBeadContext::default()` into project creation, but `create_project_from_bead_context` still validates a contract-marked override's `## Nearby work` against `render_bead_task_prompt(context)`. In the real CLI path that comparison is therefore against an empty/default nearby section, so a canonical override that preserves the actual graph-derived nearby body will be rejected.
- `fr-23-45b2589f`: Accept
  The core issue is real: for direct nearby relations, both filtering and rendered status use `detail -> relation.status -> summary`, so when detail refresh is unavailable a stale non-closed relation status can override a closed/deferred target summary and keep a dead item in `## Nearby work`. The writeup overstates the `relation.status == None` case, because missing relation status does fall through to summary, but the precedence bug still exists for stale populated relation metadata.
- `fr-23-c7ef5b17`: Accept
  Reviewer guidance is built from `extract_planned_elsewhere_routing_bead_ids`, which can include short aliases, while merge-time canonicalization independently rejects aliases that map to multiple canonical IDs. In the described mixed `Already Planned Elsewhere` plus same-short-id nearby case, guidance can list `bead-4` as valid even though `PlannedElsewhereIdCanonicalizer::from_prompt` marks it ambiguous and drops it. That is a correctness and UX mismatch between prompt contract and merge behavior.
